text: Using a combination of first-principle calculations and deformation potential theory, we explored intrinsic carrier mobility of the monolayer MoS2 sheet and nanoribbons. In contrast with large reduction in carrier mobility in graphene nanoribbons in contrast to that of graphene sheet, intrinsic mobility in armchair nanoribbons of MoS2 was found to be almost the same with MoS2 sheet and shows oscillations with ribbon width. We also observed a room temperature transport polarity reversal in both the sheet and nanoribbons with hole mobility (μh) being 200.52 cm2 V(-1) s(-1) and electron mobility (μe) is 72.16 cm2 V(-1) s(-1) in sheet and μh as 49.72 cm2 V(-1) s(-1) and μe as 190.89 cm2 V(-1) s(-1) in 4 nm wide nanoribbon. The robust intrinsic mobility with polarity reversal in MoS2 nanostructures is attributed to the variation of different types of edge states along the width of nanoribbons. Our findings indicate that in terms of width reduction of nanostructures along with edge engineering may facilitate transport in MoS2 nanostructures.
explanation: The passage is highly technical and reads like a research abstract; minor stylistic oddities and repetition hint at AI authorship, though the content is plausible as human-written.
